reviews.review_description:
Virtual Fighting Championship is not your typical VR brawler — here, your opponents are other VR users just like you, complete with relatable backstories and virtual quirks. Fight your way through a humorous and intense lineup: from a hated office manager, to a gamer geek, to his vengeful girlfriend — each battle feels like part of a satirical VR world with real personality.

The combat is highly physical and cardio-heavy: duck, dodge, block, and throw combos to survive. Movement is fluid and responsive, making the experience feel incredibly immersive. The environments are stylish and believable, and every punch lands with satisfying impact.

Whether you're looking to burn some calories or blow off steam, this game delivers both fun and fitness. Highly recommended for fans of VR action with a twist! 💥
i like this game a lot so i can't give it below 3 stars.  but wth?  campaign saved only up to 2-4 on the first guy.  can't get it to save beyond that, or for any of the others.  please fix.

developer_response.body: Hi! Apologize for the late response. The bug has been fixed in the latest build. I've also added "Visual Clean" mode for players who want less data but more immersion. Cheers!

reviews.review_description: The game developers are active in improving the game and in the community. The community is generally very kind and accepting. And the game itself, even if not the most realistic, is incredibly fun and still very good cardio.

reviews.review_title: One of the best fighting games out there
reviews.review_description: My gateway game into pvp VR fighting, very good experience. Helped me lose 20 pounds. A must have for any competitive enjoyer of VR fighting games.

Enjoyable to play, would like shorter wait times between opponents in single player mode to keep the heartrate up. Maybe a bypass choice to quickly go from fight to fight. And leg trackers would be amazing...

reviews.review_title: Pretty fun for the first hour
reviews.review_description: It's a good game. The endless robots mode is by far the most fun as there's not too much going on in your face. The auto-matchmaking is not good. The punch tracking is terrible. I have nothing else to say. Weird discord community too

Coming from Thrill I was excited to try this. 

I was trying to be careful about my boundaries but very soon punched a picture on the wall very hard and broke it. If there is no option to play in a fixed space (and keep the opponent in the same space so I don't reach out of it!) I just can't use this. I move too much with my feet and do not want to use the joystick.

Cool idea and animations / sound tho. I didn't get a great read on framerate but it seemed just a little stuttery. I would do everything to optimize framerate in a game like this. Or any game for that matter.. smooth fps makes an important impression on people.

Unfortunately I have to return this one.
perfect game

reviews.review_title: The best pvp game out there
reviews.review_description: Trust me when I say this this is the best arcadey pvp game there is on vr, like there are mechanics to it, fun gameplay, a hell of a stamina test, and if you want fast fights and a lot of knock outs you can force that! This is the best game ever

reviews.review_title: a must have for any fighting game lovers
reviews.review_description: if you’re still waiting for totf pvp and didn’t know about this game. you’re missing out big time. this game is much more  ‘hardcore’ than it looks. while this is advertised as a street fighter style fighting game, but the in built mechanics are all grounded in realistic dynamics. and i’ve been nothing but impressed with what i have experienced so far. and the discord community is very lively so finding a fight isn’t hard at all consider this game still have a relatively small player pool (which is why i want to share this game with more people)
This game is such a hidden gem. I would definitely go with it whenever I need to relieve a bit of stress. Highly recommended.

reviews.review_title: Best PvP boxing game I’ve come across!
reviews.review_description: Best PvP boxing game I’ve come across!
This app has to be hands down the most constructive way to blow off some stem, plus at the same time get a good workout in😀😀😀🤙🏻

reviews.review_title: On par with the greatest, includes PvP
reviews.review_description:
VFC leaves a good impression at first sight. Pretty visuals, good controls, very active game... It is something you easily get into and start having fun without a long learning curve to get you started.

Offering a good first fighting game like creed and thrill of the fight, the game add on top of that a PvP mode.

With various game modes like fighting waves of enemies, fighting a campaign mode that offers a ladder style tournament (similar to mortal kombat)  where you pick a character and try to take on the best fighters (AI ones) till you reach the top!
The PvP addition is very welcomed. Fight your friends inside of the cage and see who is the better fighter!

You don't want to miss that game. Grab the game, a sweat band and show your friends what you're made of!

reviews.review_title: Very good and will just get better
reviews.review_description:
I end up spending 80% of my time on Oculus doing some sort of boxing, whether it’s Thrill of the Fight, Les Mills, or Supernatural. Intrigued by this game since unlike the others it has a PvP feature so I took a chance on it.

I am really enjoying it so far. The single fighting vs. AI is fun and it’s shorter so if I don’t want to do a long TOTF fight I have a shorter alternative that’s enjoyable. 

The PvP is pretty awesome. This game is not so much a boxing simulation like TOTF but more like a Street Fighter-type game but it’s a hell of a lot of fun and just one fight at three 3-minute rounds is a great workout. 

It’s a bit difficult to find online fighting partners but they have a really good group on Discord and everyone I have met has been really nice. I tend to be an introvert and don’t like interacting much with other people on games but everyone I have fought online or interacted with on Discord has been great, and very helpful to a beginner. 

As this game gets more users I expect it to be easier to find online fighting partners. Either way it gets five stars from me. Can’t wait for new features from the developer as time goes on.

reviews.review_title: Best fighting PVP game in VR
reviews.review_description:
I've been playing this game with my kid for a while now on steam. 3 rounds get your drenched in sweat and the action feels right. Now the single player is out with awesome style, locations and mechanics!
The characters got that street fighter feel, and the single player can be challenging.
You can't just aimlessly throw punches or swing your arms, as the AI will block and adapt.
Been waiting for a while for this game to come to quest and very happy it's finally here!

reviews.review_title: The best fighting game hands down
reviews.review_description:
Simply amazing. I think this is raised the bar for the whole fighting game genre in VR previously held by totf. Alot of innovative solution to the hard problems in fighting games.

Now, is this a better totf? Well this is not a boxing game, so it's like comparing UFC to Street fighter, but the game offer almost the same if not more nuanced fighting mechanics and give the realism to fighting. (VFC looks gamey but only in visual style, the game core itself is very realistic)

This game has a very different but equally solid single player compared to totf and at the same time the PvP is simply amazing, had ton of fun playing with my bro from a world away and we fought at 200ms, but the game still feels super smooth at this latency which is voodoo magic to me. I cant imagine how fun the game would be if the pings are at a normal level.

The game is super responsive to detecting your swing, the damage output is shown to you so you know precisely how much damage you dealt, it visibility rewards swing speed and form. So wiggling is by nature not encouraged.

The game's mechanics against spamming punches (creed had the silly stamina system) is it's stun system. If you get hit hard, you get stunned, and there is a broken screen effect signalling this effect in action. During this stun period your own bits are rendered useless, so you are naturally incentivise to stop attacks and start defending, I find this solution very elegant and should be the standard for other games to follow.

Also watched the devlog and notice the head roll system they implemented, so it take into account your head movement when you get hit, and if your are rolling away from the lunch, you receive less damage, this is remarkable level of attention to detail, unmatched by any other fighting games on the market.

The game is fun and everything I like the characters I hope it's more characters you can play with I'm not sure if they got any powers or anything but what I don't like is on the main menu and you do your selection on the quest it starts glitching or when you first get to the main menu once I'd be good stop getting on popup RV switch to a training and all that stuff section it was glitch and be blurry for a minute did clear up please fix that but all so far the game is fun I just started playing but we will get a workout promise you that

VFC has been around for a while on PC, but this port to quest is just fantastic. It's definitely more arcade style fighting but sometimes that'll scratch the itch better than anything else. 

Are you a fan of boxing? What about street fighter? If you said yes to either, you should ask yourself why this isn't in your library yet.

For the price it is not worth more than 1 stars.  Sincerely, you sell dreams with your trailer but the game is quite different, quite empty in content (and apparently according to another comment, it will not evolve).  The training could be good, especially with the robots, but why not have him attack?  the adventure mode is so messy that in the end we type in all directions since we have no visibility due to the popups that are permanently displayed on the opponent when we touch him, so we see nothing (why  not displaying them at the top of the screen without disrupting the fight or the visuals. This is nonsense).  and finally you have to create an account to save some of these progress, why?  including a save in the game is too much to ask?  certainly as you mention, it's not toft but be consistent with your trailer and give us a real arcade game like others do like creed or knockout league which combines arcade and boxing very well.
But toft remains the best, and yes I compare this game to toft because when I buy a game called Vitual Fighting Championship, I expect to fight opponents in the arena with a minimum of realism and not the other way around

developer_response.body:
Hey there

This is the first time ever VFC has received a complaint about our content being empty, as we have arguably one of the most abundant trainings and game modes in the genre.

VFC is built from the ground up for its PvP game-play. Everything else is supplementary to this core mode, so if you didn't get to experience VFC's PvP, Would love to welcome you to our wonderful fighting community inside our discord at vfcgame.com/discord.

We have players who have been fighting in VFC for more than 3 years and saw the game grow from a bare bone fighting experience to the game that it is now. And we have never received complaints about the game being stagnant or not being actively updated.

The robot trainer you mentioned is purely a training tool provided to players as a warm up tool, each mini-game inside the training area serves one purpose. For AI fighting, we have a dedicated single player campaign mode where the AI does fight back and fight back well. The AI can detect players' fighting patterns and adjust their defence accordingly.

We will soon add an option to let players toggle off damage pop-ups so those players who prefer a more clean and minimalistic GUI can get that as an option.

We do not compare ourselves to TOTF because we don't see VFC as a 'Boxing' only game, VFC will allow players to fight in whatever martial art style they prefer, and will in the future add things such as elbow strikes and eye jabs.

I have every confidence to deliver a very high standard of fighting realism while not losing the 'fun' in gaming.

Lastly, the game has always evolved since 2017 and WILL keep evolving in the future (there is a devlog series keeping track of all our development progress on our youtube channel), no idea about your 'another comment' that is referred to?

reviews.review_title: A nice alternative to Creed and TotF
reviews.review_description:
This is a great workout game with some challenging but fun AI and excellent game for PvP (expect a small player base though so you'll have to join the Discord). The recent addition of a traditional campaign really brought me in. 

This title has improved a lot since I played it a few years ago on Viveport. It's similar to Thrill of the Fight but you can locomote with the joystick and have specific combos you can throw to activate super attacks (think Street Fighter). There are only a few characters on the roster but each has pros and cons so you'll want to try them all to find one that fits your play style. 

There are training modes, campaign, multiplayer, and some modes similar to Gorn where you fight a small amount of enemies at once. The game calculates how hard you punch for power and reduces damage if you're blocking or "rolling with the punch." You'll be covered in sweat in no time but love every second.

My only complaint is that you must make an account to save your single player progress even. I get it for cross platform multiplayer but single player, that shouldn't be required.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind word. It means a lot to me! The account-based progressing system was designed for family members to share the game like the Nintendo Switch. Buy maybe I over complicated things...

reviews.review_description:
This game has a really good workout potential and is really fun! , the ability to fight in multiplayer makes it a real sport !  also this is not just punch randomly  if you can manage to do a punch combo on your opponent the game rewards you with more damage, which is really fun and keeps you trying ! 

This game is awesome and the community around it too

A very good PVP focused game. Running on Q2 very smooth, had no problem with tracking. 
There is a nice community on discord server where you can find opponents, dev is active and responsive for any feedback. 
Also you can try yourself in a singleplayer campaign which is a good addition for training your skill.
The characters have some skills which you can use in the fight but the pure boxing mode is in development right now, which I'm looking forward to.
Overall I recommend this game if you prefer PVP fighting over singleplayer and if you are into boxing games 🙂

I've played this game for a while since early access and I can honestly say it is the best player vs player fighting experience you can get in VR. A thoughtful blend of fitness and combat simulation with fighting game concepts and mechanics. The dev team clerly spent time thinking about how different mechnics work, rewarding both real life fight experience and athleticism as well as video gaming skill. It strikes a very good balance between the two and really becomes its own VR esport. Recently added a single player mode and has a great online discord for pvp fights.

reviews.review_title: Enjoy TOFT but want something new?
reviews.review_description: This gets close. I understand that compensating for multiplayer won't let it feel as clean as TOFT, but they have a great product thus far. I put it as a  'maybe buy' -- although the foundation is solid, the lack of significant updates on PC (owned for a year and a half) has me apprehensive about the developer following through.
I had so much fun playing this game! I didn’t realise the time and was already fighting and sweating like crazy!! It is the best fighting game for multiplayers!

Not sure where these 5/5 reviews have come from,.but for me this doesn't come close to The Thrill of the Fight.

When I was playing I felt I wasn't in a fight like I do with The Thrill of the Fight. Just doesn't feel right to me. Just wasn't for me at all.

The perfect 5/5 reviews on a brand new game made my mind up to purchase, but after playing I will not make that mistake again. 

If you have The Thrill of the Fight (or Creed - which I haven't played), you may find this a step down from it. Just my opinion though.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback! Aside of being a more PVP focused game, the design philosophies between VFC and your classic Boxing games are different.

We are very confidence in VFC's core capability in tracking player's fighting technique and punching power and form, but we do add fantasy elements into the game, and maybe a crazier graphical style, so it's down to different preference of styles :)

reviews.review_description:
My first positive association is naturally Virtua Fighter. If you expect "Thrill of The Fight" in multiplayer, you may be negatively surprised. VFC stands between simulation and arcade. It's not perfect, but the joy of smacking a friend's face in a PvP game is irreplaceable ;). Rating? Very strong 4.5.
The game has great community, you must have join their Discord.
Combine your daily training with slapping the virtual face of your friends or people around the world!
This is what fighting games needed in the quest, it has a lot of fitness modes and you can beat the crap out of your friends with the online pvp, that’s a winner for me

Tried it twice don't care for it. Well made tho. It's a quality game i prefer totf tho. Others seem to love it. So... Is there a refund policy if you just simply don't like a game? It works nothing wrong with it. I just don't like it.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! Aside of being a more PVP focused game, the design philosophies between VFC and classic Boxing games are different, so it does come down to a personal preference i guess. but i would love to hear more from you about what we can improvement in increase your chances of liking VFC!

reviews.review_description:
As it is its very choppy
And pvp is unplayable
I'll rate it again at full release right now its...
Kind of a let down

I would like to see better graphics come along 
Maybe some grapple action to throw them around
Wouldn't recommend this app - not fun to play.

developer_response.body: My last reddit post was over 100 days ago. If you have gameplay complaints or suggestions you are more than welcome to post them here or on the discord server. But punishing a dedicated indie for other people's doing isn't cool.
